definition: fetch is the built-in JavaScript API for making HTTP requests. It returns a Promise that resolves to a Response object, from which you read the body (e.g. .json()). Crucially, it only rejects on network failure — not on HTTP error statuses.
layman: fetch is how JavaScript asks another server for data over the internet. You send a request, wait for the reply, and read it — but you have to check yourself whether the reply was actually a success.
content:
## fetch & API calls

`fetch(url, options)` returns a `Promise<Response>`. You `await` it, then read the body.

### A GET

```ts
const res = await fetch("https://api.example.com/models")
if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
const data = await res.json()
```

### The #1 gotcha: fetch does NOT reject on 4xx/5xx

A 404 or 500 still **fulfills** the Promise. `fetch` only rejects on a *network* error (DNS, offline, CORS). So you must check **`res.ok`** (or `res.status`) yourself and throw.

### A POST to an LLM API

```ts
const res = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
  method: "POST",
  headers: {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({ model, messages }),
})
```

Key pieces: `method`, `headers` (content type + `Authorization: Bearer <key>`), and a JSON-stringified `body`.

### Timeouts & cancellation

Use an `AbortController` to cancel a slow request:

```ts
const ctrl = new AbortController()
setTimeout(() => ctrl.abort(), 10_000)
await fetch(url, { signal: ctrl.signal })
```

### Streaming

For token streaming, read `response.body` as a stream instead of calling `.json()`, processing chunks (often Server-Sent Events) as they arrive.

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Many Gen AI integrations call REST endpoints directly: you POST a prompt with a Bearer key, check `res.ok` to catch rate limits (429) and errors, parse the JSON reply, stream tokens via `response.body`, and bound calls with `AbortController` timeouts.
keyPoints: fetch returns Promise<Response>; await it, then read the body (.json()/.text()).
fetch only rejects on network errors — always check res.ok for HTTP 4xx/5xx.
POST needs method, headers (Content-Type + Authorization: Bearer), and a JSON.stringify body.
Use AbortController (signal) to add timeouts and cancellation.
Stream tokens by reading response.body instead of awaiting .json().
